items.title: Waymo recalls nearly 4,000 robotaxis to stop them driving into highway construction zones
items.topic: Waymo recalls nearly 4,000 robotaxis to stop them driving into highway construction zones
items.summary: Waymo has recalled nearly 4,000 of its robotaxis due to issues with the vehicles driving into highway construction zones. The recall highlights the ongoing challenges in ensuring the safety and reliability of autonomous vehicles in complex real-world scenarios.
items.abstract: Waymo has recalled nearly 4,000 of its robotaxis due to issues with the vehicles driving into highway construction zones. The recall highlights the ongoing challenges in ensuring the safety and reliability of autonomous vehicles in complex real-world scenarios.

items.title: EXCLUSIVE: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving tech in Europe over speeding concerns
items.topic: EXCLUSIVE: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving tech in Europe over speeding concerns
items.summary: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving technology in Europe over concerns about speeding. This development underscores the regulatory scrutiny and safety concerns surrounding Tesla's autonomous driving features in international markets.
items.abstract: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving technology in Europe over concerns about speeding. This development underscores the regulatory scrutiny and safety concerns surrounding Tesla's autonomous driving features in international markets.

items.title: Fine-tuned multimodal large language model for autonomous state cognition system of shape-recognition 6-bar tensegrity integrated with flexible sensors
items.topic: Fine-tuned multimodal large language model for autonomous state cognition system of shape-recognition 6-bar tensegrity integrated with flexible sensors
items.summary: A fine-tuned multimodal large language model has been developed for an autonomous state cognition system of a shape-recognition 6-bar tensegrity integrated with flexible sensors. This research advances the integration of LLMs with physical systems for enhanced perception and cognition in autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: A fine-tuned multimodal large language model has been developed for an autonomous state cognition system of a shape-recognition 6-bar tensegrity integrated with flexible sensors. This research advances the integration of LLMs with physical systems for enhanced perception and cognition in autonomous vehicles.

items.title: Tesla presented misleading ‘Full Self-Driving’ safety data to European regulators
items.topic: Tesla presented misleading ‘Full Self-Driving’ safety data to European regulators
items.summary: Tesla has been accused of presenting misleading safety data for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology to European regulators. This revelation raises significant concerns about the transparency and reliability of Tesla's autonomous driving claims.
items.abstract: Tesla has been accused of presenting misleading safety data for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology to European regulators. This revelation raises significant concerns about the transparency and reliability of Tesla's autonomous driving claims.

items.title: US senators ask for review of Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' safety data, citing Reuters report
items.topic: US senators ask for review of Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' safety data, citing Reuters report
items.summary: US senators have requested a review of Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) safety data, citing a Reuters report. The senators' action highlights the ongoing scrutiny and regulatory attention on Tesla's autonomous driving capabilities.
items.abstract: US senators have requested a review of Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) safety data, citing a Reuters report. The senators' action highlights the ongoing scrutiny and regulatory attention on Tesla's autonomous driving capabilities.

items.title: Tesla teases new FSD features — but it’s still not the self-driving promised
items.topic: Tesla teases new FSD features — but it’s still not the self-driving promised
items.summary: Tesla has teased new features for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, but the company acknowledges that the technology is still not the fully autonomous driving solution it has promised. This update reflects the incremental progress and ongoing limitations of Tesla's FSD technology.
items.abstract: Tesla has teased new features for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, but the company acknowledges that the technology is still not the fully autonomous driving solution it has promised. This update reflects the incremental progress and ongoing limitations of Tesla's FSD technology.

items.title: Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
items.topic: Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
items.summary: Google DeepMind is investing in multi-agent AI safety research to address the challenges of ensuring safe and reliable interactions between multiple AI systems. This initiative is crucial for the development of autonomous vehicles and other complex AI applications.
items.abstract: Google DeepMind is investing in multi-agent AI safety research to address the challenges of ensuring safe and reliable interactions between multiple AI systems. This initiative is crucial for the development of autonomous vehicles and other complex AI applications.

items.title: RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation
items.topic: RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation
items.summary: The RT-VLA model, proposed by researchers on arXiv, enables real-time vision-language-action processing for autonomous driving by distilling the knowledge of the state-of-the-art SimLingo model into a compact student model, achieving a significant reduction in inference latency while preserving language-based reasoning and explainability, with potential applications in safety-critical driving scenarios, as of June 12, 2026. This advancement is crucial for the development of autonomous vehicles that can operate safely and efficiently in complex environments. The RT-VLA model's ability to provide post-hoc explanations for its decisions makes it a valuable tool for improving the trustworthiness of autonomous vehicles. With its real-time capabilities, RT-VLA can be used to enhance the decision-making processes of autonomous vehicles, leading to improved safety and performance on the road.

items.title: DriveJudge: Rethinking Autonomous Driving Evaluation with Vision-Language Models
items.topic: DriveJudge: Rethinking Autonomous Driving Evaluation with Vision-Language Models
items.summary: DriveJudge, a vision-language model-based driving evaluation agent proposed on arXiv, combines rule-grounded evaluation with VLM reasoning to assess driving quality in a context-aware and interpretable manner, addressing the challenges of evaluating autonomous driving policies, as of June 15, 2026. DriveJudge's ability to selectively invoke physically-grounded deterministic rule functions enables it to provide more accurate and reliable evaluations of driving performance. The model's performance was validated using a curated dataset, demonstrating its potential to improve the evaluation and development of autonomous driving systems. By providing a more comprehensive understanding of driving quality, DriveJudge can help to identify areas for improvement and optimize the performance of autonomous vehicles. With its unique approach to driving evaluation, DriveJudge has the potential to become a valuable tool for the development of safer and more efficient autonomous vehicles.

items.title: ParkingTransformer: LLM-Enhanced End-to-End Trajectory Planning for Autonomous Parking
items.topic: ParkingTransformer: LLM-Enhanced End-to-End Trajectory Planning for Autonomous Parking
items.summary: ParkingTransformer, a novel framework proposed on arXiv, leverages multi-view perception and large language models to enable end-to-end autonomous parking, providing high-level semantic understanding and interpretability, and eliminating the need for dense Bird's-View representations, as of June 12, 2026. This framework has the potential to improve the efficiency and safety of autonomous parking systems, which is a critical component of autonomous driving. By combining trajectory queries with LLMs' implicit state features, ParkingTransformer can interact directly with historical information and raw sensor data to output planning trajectories. The model's ability to provide interpretable results makes it a valuable tool for improving the trustworthiness of autonomous parking systems. With its end-to-end approach, ParkingTransformer can be used to optimize the parking process, reducing the need for human intervention and improving the overall efficiency of autonomous vehicles.
